hero.title: Executive briefings with thesis, context, and consequences.
hero.lede: A sober visual base for turning signals from technology, AI, and the economy into a compact strategic read.

topLine.title: Distribution is becoming as important as the model.
topLine.body: Advantage no longer lives only in training or accessing better models. It increasingly lives in controlling interface, workflow, user context, and distribution. Whoever packages intelligence best captures the user relationship and, with it, the margin.

items.label: Models
items.text: Raw model differentiation is narrowing; distribution matters more.

items.label: Product
items.text: Integrated suites are gaining ground against standalone tools.

items.label: Market
items.text: Pressure is moving toward bundles rather than isolated features.

items.label: Work
items.text: System orchestration matters more than proficiency with a single model.

deepDives.title: Three angles for reading the shift

items.title: 1. The battleground is moving
items.body: As models become relatively more commoditized, competition shifts toward product integration, workflow control, and usage frequency. That redistributes power away from the pure lab and toward the operator who owns the working surface.

items.title: 2. Value is concentrating in context
items.body: The scarce asset is no longer inference alone. It is structured context: memory, proprietary data, user habits, and embedding inside real operating systems for work.

items.title: 3. Interface determines capture
items.body: Interface is not cosmetic. It shapes what gets measured, repeated, remembered, and monetized. The presentation layer ends up influencing the economics of the product itself.

marketMap.title: Who benefits if this thesis is right

items.label: Winners
items.text: Platforms with distribution, data, and daily user surface area.

items.label: Pressured
items.text: Vendors whose moat depended mainly on access to a better model.

items.label: Opportunity
items.text: Vertical products that turn AI into concrete workflow.
Which layer captures the recurring user relationship?
Where does the most valuable and least portable context accumulate?
Which products turn occasional use into operational dependency?
